zone_name: Salt Lake – Rajarhat (IT Corridor / New Town)

geography.data_source: KMC ward map + NKDA (New Town Kolkata Development Authority) boundary, Salt Lake Sector V and Rajarhat New Town delineation

demographics.data_source: Census 2011 North 24 Parganas district tables (Salt Lake/Rajarhat area), rapid growth at 5.8% CAGR in New Town, NKDA population estimates

income_profile.data_source: NSSO 78th Round (2020-21) West Bengal urban tables, NASSCOM Bengal IT industry data, Sector V tech park employment, PLFS 2022-23

commute_profile.data_source: Kolkata Metro Green Line (East-West Corridor) partial operation data 2023, planned stations for Salt Lake/Rajarhat extension, AC bus service data

sensitivity_parameters.data_source: Estimated from mid-high income IT corridor, low fare sensitivity, high digital diffusion, lower political mobilisation than old Kolkata due to migrant professional population

social_network_parameters.data_source: Estimated from IT corridor and new township patterns, workplace networks dominant, weaker neighbourhood bonds than old Kolkata

informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from Sector V tech park food court and restaurant cluster footfall
Estimated from City Centre New Town / Salt Lake commercial hub activity
Estimated from New Town planned commercial zone, NKDA commercial area survey

tier1_agent_archetype_weights.data_source: Adjusted IT Corridor template for Salt Lake/Rajarhat — reduced tech_knowledge_worker from 0.52 to 0.38 (Kolkata IT sector smaller than Bangalore/Hyderabad), increased formal_sector_employee and government (West Bengal government offices in Salt Lake)

tier2_agent_types.data_source: Estimated from NKDA governance, NASSCOM Bengal chapter, RWA presence in planned townships
